title: Think Tanks, Ideological Infrastructure & the Political Money Behind Policy

overview.summary: Think tanks and ideological infrastructure organizations serve as the intellectual engines of American politics, translating donor money into policy proposals, judicial nominations, model legislation, and media narratives. The combined annual budgets of the major conservative and liberal think tank networks exceed $1 billion, with dark money networks on both sides channeling billions more per election cycle. This chapter maps the financial flows from wealthy donors through think tanks, dark money networks, and media infrastructure that shape American governance.
keyFindings: Dark money spending hit a record $1.9 billion in the 2024 federal election cycle, roughly double the 2020 total
The Arabella Advisors liberal dark money network processed $1.51 billion in revenue in 2024 alone
Leonard Leo's conservative legal network received a single $1.6 billion donation from Barre Seid in 2021, the largest known political advocacy donation in U.S. history
DonorsTrust has granted more than $2.5 billion to conservative causes since its inception
The Koch network's AFP Action super PAC spent $157 million in 2024 federal elections, tripling its 2020 level
ALEC model bills are introduced approximately 1,000 times per year in state legislatures, with roughly 20% enacted into law
Pink slime partisan news sites (1,265) now outnumber real daily newspapers (1,213) in the United States
Combined conservative think tank budgets (Heritage, AEI, Cato, Manhattan, Hoover) exceed $400 million annually
Project 2025 grew to 100+ coalition partner organizations with 280+ contributors

conservativeThinkTankNetwork.title: Conservative Think Tank Network
conservativeThinkTankNetwork.overview: The major conservative think tanks form an interconnected ecosystem that translates donor money into policy proposals, personnel pipelines, and intellectual frameworks for governance. Their combined annual budgets exceed $400 million.

heritageFoundation.name: Heritage Foundation

project2025.description: Comprehensive conservative governance blueprint modeled after the original 1981 Mandate for Leadership
project2025.contributors: 280+ named contributors (NYT traced 307; 182 tied to Trump)
project2025.fundingFromBillionaireFamilies: $120 million+ flowing into advisory groups since 2020

mandateForLeadership1981.reaganAdoptionRate: Nearly two-thirds of 2,000 proposals adopted or attempted
mandateForLeadership1981.impact: Reagan distributed copies at first cabinet meeting; NYT called it 'the manifesto of the Reagan revolution'
mandateForLeadership1981.firstYearAdoption: 60% of proposals implemented or initiated by end of Reagan's first year

policyInfluence: Tax policy (2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act framework)

catoInstitute.name: Cato Institute
catoInstitute.foundedAs: Charles Koch Foundation (articles of incorporation filed December 1974)

hooverInstitution.name: Hoover Institution on War, Revolution and Peace

liberalProgressiveThinkTankNetwork.title: Liberal/Progressive Think Tank Network
liberalProgressiveThinkTankNetwork.overview: Progressive think tanks collectively operate with significant budgets focused on social policy, economic inequality, health care, and environmental regulation. Their combined annual budgets approach $300 million.

majorDonors.sorosOrganizations2005to2010: $5.4 million+ (via Foundation to Support Open Society and Open Society Institute)

brookingsInstitution.name: Brookings Institution
brookingsInstitution.fundingProfile: More than 80% from gifts and grants; diverse array of individuals, foundations, corporations, and governments

economicPolicyInstitute.name: Economic Policy Institute (EPI)
economicPolicyInstitute.note: Strong ties to organized labor; union-funded research on minimum wage, trade policy, and worker protections

kochNetwork.title: Koch Political Network
kochNetwork.overview: The Koch network is the most extensive privately funded political operation in American history, spanning donor summits, super PACs, dark money nonprofits, think tanks, and grassroots advocacy organizations. Total spending has ranged from $400 million to $900 million per election cycle.

history.foundingPeriod: Charles Koch began building political infrastructure in the 1970s, co-founding the Cato Institute in 1977

spendingByElectionCycle.2018: $400 million announced budget (60% increase over 2016 cycle)
spendingByElectionCycle.2024: $157 million+ through AFP Action super PAC alone

americansForProsperity.name: Americans for Prosperity (AFP)
americansForProsperity.spending2024Breakdown: Two-thirds went to House and Senate races

majorFunding2024.kochIndustries: $40 million (two donations in May 2023 and July 2024)

standTogether.name: Stand Together (formerly Seminar Network)
standTogether.description: Koch's primary philanthropic network channeling funds to policy organizations
standTogether.standTogetherTrustContributions2022: $5 million to SPN-affiliated think tanks, plus millions more to ALEC and Cato
standTogether.chamberOfCommerceRole: Served as dark money conduit for $43 million to AFP Action in 2024

freedomPartners.name: Freedom Partners Chamber of Commerce
freedomPartners.description: Koch network's primary fundraising hub (active 2012-2019), now largely replaced by Stand Together Chamber of Commerce
freedomPartners.historicalRole: Served as fiscal hub distributing donor summit funds to network organizations

influenceAreas: Energy policy and fossil fuel deregulation
Tax policy (estate tax elimination, corporate rate cuts)
Higher education funding (Mercatus Center, university programs)
kochNetwork.totalKochPoliticalSpending: $2+ billion cumulative across election cycles since 2008

arabellaAdvisorsNetwork.title: Arabella Advisors / Liberal Dark Money Network
arabellaAdvisorsNetwork.overview: Arabella Advisors (now Sunflower Services) manages the largest liberal dark money network in America, operating through five primary nonprofit entities that function as fiscal sponsors for hundreds of pop-up advocacy groups. Combined revenue reached $1.51 billion in 2024.

parentCompany.name: Arabella Advisors LLC (rebranded as Sunflower Services)
parentCompany.description: Advises left-leaning donors and nonprofits about where to give money; manages five dark money nonprofits

newVentureFund.name: New Venture Fund
newVentureFund.role: Primary fiscal sponsor for charitable projects and pop-up organizations

sixteenThirtyFund.name: Sixteen Thirty Fund
sixteenThirtyFund.role: Political advocacy arm; described by The Atlantic as 'the indisputable heavyweight of Democratic dark money'

windwardFund.name: Windward Fund
windwardFund.role: Fiscal sponsor for environmental and conservation projects

financials.cumulativeDescription: The network has channeled billions in untraceable funds to progressive causes since 2016

hansjoergWyss.name: Hansjoerg Wyss (Swiss billionaire)
hansjoergWyss.totalDonations: $245 million to Sixteen Thirty Fund and New Venture Fund since 2016

gatesFoundation.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
gatesFoundation.totalFunding: $450 million over sixteen years (one of earliest and largest funders)

fiscalSponsorshipModel.description: Arabella's nonprofits serve as fiscal sponsors for hundreds of temporary advocacy organizations ('pop-up groups') that appear to be independent but operate under the financial and legal umbrella of the parent nonprofits
fiscalSponsorshipModel.mechanism: Pop-up groups receive tax-exempt status, payroll, and financial infrastructure from the parent fund without separate incorporation
fiscalSponsorshipModel.advantage: Donor anonymity preserved; groups can appear and disappear as campaigns require; no separate IRS filings

donorsTrustAndDonorsCapitalFund.title: DonorsTrust & Donors Capital Fund
donorsTrustAndDonorsCapitalFund.overview: DonorsTrust and its sister organization Donors Capital Fund serve as the primary donor-advised fund infrastructure for conservative and libertarian donors, earning the nickname 'dark money ATM of the right' from Mother Jones magazine. The organizations allow anonymous giving to conservative causes through a double-blind mechanism.

donorsCapitalFund.name: Donors Capital Fund
donorsCapitalFund.description: Sister organization handling larger anonymous donations

mechanism.description: Donors make tax-deductible contributions to DonorsTrust, which then distributes grants in its own name, concealing the identity of the original donor
mechanism.anonymityFunction: Creates a 'murky money maze' where original donors are untraceable because grants are distributed under the DonorsTrust/DCF name

majorRecipientCategories: Conservative think tanks (Heritage, AEI, Cato, Manhattan Institute)
School choice and education reform groups
Federalist Society and Leonard Leo network
donorsTrustAndDonorsCapitalFund.kochConnection: Serves as key intermediary for Koch network donor funds; connected to State Policy Network and ALEC funding

alec.title: ALEC (American Legislative Exchange Council)
alec.overview: ALEC is the primary mechanism through which corporations directly collaborate with state legislators to draft model legislation, effectively outsourcing lawmaking to corporate interests. With nearly 300 corporate members and one-quarter of state legislators, ALEC bills are introduced approximately 1,000 times per year with a 20% enactment rate.

financials.donorGrowth2022: 55% increase in new donors; 45% increase in individual funder support

membership.corporateMembers: Nearly 300 corporate, foundation, and private-sector members
membership.legislativeMembers: Claims nearly one-quarter of state legislators; CMD records show 897 members in early 2020
membership.membershipFee: Legislators pay nominal $100 annual dues; corporations pay $7,000-$25,000+ for membership

modelLegislation.historicalTrack2010to2018: 2,900 bills introduced in all 50 states and Congress; 600+ became law
policyAreas: Preemption of local minimum wage and labor laws

corporateLegislatorRelationship.description: Corporations and legislators sit as equals on ALEC task forces, jointly voting on model legislation that legislators then introduce in their home states
corporateLegislatorRelationship.mechanism: ALEC provides template bills, talking points, and legislative strategy; corporations fund the process and shape the language
corporateLegislatorRelationship.taskForces: Organized by policy area (tax, energy, education, criminal justice, etc.)

majorCorporateFunders.pharmaIndustry: Multiple pharmaceutical companies on Private Enterprise Board

trayvonMartinExodus2012.trigger: Public outcry over ALEC's role in promoting Stand Your Ground laws after the Trayvon Martin shooting
trayvonMartinExodus2012.corporateExits: 25 corporations dropped ALEC membership, plus 4 major nonprofits and 55 elected officials
trayvonMartinExodus2012.financialImpact: Lost $1.4 million in income by June 2013; budget hole exceeded one-third of projected income
trayvonMartinExodus2012.recoveryEffort: 'Prodigal Son Project' — ALEC identified 40+ lapsed corporate members to recruit back

alec.tiestoSPN: All 64 SPN member state think tanks have pushed ALEC agenda; 34+ have direct ties to ALEC

federalistSocietyAndLeonardLeoNetwork.title: Federalist Society & Leonard Leo Network
federalistSocietyAndLeonardLeoNetwork.overview: The Federalist Society serves as the gateway through which conservative legal ideology is translated into judicial appointments, regulatory policy, and law school culture. Leonard Leo, its longtime co-chairman, has built a parallel dark money empire exceeding $1.6 billion that extends far beyond judicial nominations into media, culture, and policy.

federalistSociety.name: Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies
federalistSociety.founders: Founded at Yale, Harvard, and University of Chicago law schools
federalistSociety.budgetGrowth: From $100,000 and 2 student chapters to $20M+ and 200+ chapters under Leo's leadership

membership.totalMembership: Largest conservative legal organization in the U.S.

influence.judicialNominations: All 6 conservative Supreme Court justices are current or former members; vetted Trump's judicial nominee lists
influence.regulatoryPolicy: Shapes administrative law doctrine, Chevron deference challenges, non-delegation doctrine revival
influence.lawSchoolPipeline: Student chapters train next generation of conservative legal thinkers; faculty divisions embed conservative legal theory in curricula
influence.lawyerNetwork: Connects conservative attorneys across private practice, government, and judiciary

leonardLeoNetwork.overview: Leo has built a vast network of dark money organizations that has raised $600 million+ since 2014, and received a single $1.6 billion donation in 2021 — the largest known political advocacy donation in U.S. history.

barreSeidDonation.mechanism: Seid donated 100% of shares in Tripp Lite to Marble Freedom Trust, which sold stock to Eaton (Ireland-based)
barreSeidDonation.taxAvoidance: ~$400 million in capital gains taxes avoided through structure as charitable donation
barreSeidDonation.description: Largest known donation to a political advocacy group in U.S. history

marbleFreedomTrust.name: Marble Freedom Trust
marbleFreedomTrust.type: Trust (not corporation — avoids public disclosure of name, directors, address)
marbleFreedomTrust.leoPlan: Devote $1 billion to 'crush liberal dominance' in news and entertainment (Financial Times, September 2024)

the85Fund.name: The 85 Fund
the85Fund.role: Dark money node distributing funds to conservative legal and policy organizations

leonardLeoNetwork.schwabCharitableFunnel: Leo redirected hundreds of millions through a donor-advised fund at Schwab Charitable
leonardLeoNetwork.selfDealingConcerns: Leo-tied nonprofits have paid his businesses $90 million in six years (per CREW investigation)

statePolicyNetwork.title: State Policy Network (SPN)
statePolicyNetwork.overview: SPN coordinates 64 state-focused think tanks across all 50 states, forming a Koch-connected infrastructure that translates national conservative policy priorities into state-level legislation and advocacy. Combined annual revenues exceed $188 million.

kochNetworkConnections.donorsTrustAndDonorsCapitalFund: Major funding conduits for SPN members
kochNetworkConnections.charlesKochFoundation: Direct grants to multiple SPN member think tanks

influenceOnStateLegislation.alecTies: All 64 member state think tanks have pushed ALEC agenda; 34+ have direct ALEC ties
influenceOnStateLegislation.methodology: SPN members produce state-specific research to support national policy templates; coordinate with ALEC on model legislation

academicCapture.title: Academic Capture: Corporate Funding of University Research
academicCapture.overview: Corporations and wealthy donors have systematically funded university research programs, endowed chairs, and academic centers that produce research favorable to their regulatory and policy interests. The most documented case is the Koch network's multi-decade investment in George Mason University and the Mercatus Center, but the pattern extends across fossil fuel, tobacco, pharmaceutical, and food industries.

mercatusCenter.name: Mercatus Center at George Mason University
mercatusCenter.founded: Originally as Center for the Study of Market Processes at Rutgers University (founded by Richard Fink); moved to GMU in mid-1980s

kochFunding.gmuFoundationFromKochFoundations: $46.5 million since 2005 (bulk to Economics department and Law and Economics Center)

donorInfluenceOnHiring.description: Released donor agreements show Koch Foundation endowed funds for professor salaries with two of five committee members chosen by donors to select professors
donorInfluenceOnHiring.concern: Donors effectively had veto power over academic hiring decisions

transparencyIssues.privateEmailServer: Mercatus uses private email server for @mercatus.gmu.edu addresses, bypassing Virginia FOIA requirements
transparencyIssues.academicLaundering: UnKoch My Campus co-founder: 'Mercatus's ties to the university allow the organization to essentially launder its agenda so it appears more objective'

corporateInterests.google: Funded conferences at GMU where experts 'forcefully rejected' government antitrust action against Google
corporateInterests.copyrightAndPatent: Google donated to Mercatus to support research on weakening copyright and patent laws

policyInfluence: Deregulation research cited in congressional testimony
Cost-benefit analysis frameworks used to oppose environmental regulations

fossilFuelIndustryInAcademia.overview: Oil and gas companies have donated tens to hundreds of millions of dollars to colleges and universities, sat on governing boards, sponsored scholarships, and built pro-fossil fuel programming
keyFindings: Industry sponsorship creates conflicts of interest that bias research in favor of sponsors
Archival documents show fossil fuel industry deliberately uses university partnerships to further its own interests
Only 7 peer-reviewed studies scrutinize fossil fuel industry influence in academia (out of 14,000 papers on conflicts of interest)
Pattern follows playbook established by tobacco, pharmaceutical, and food industries
examples: BP sponsorship of Princeton's Climate Mitigation Initiative
Shell's funding of Enhanced Learning Fund at University of Alberta

tobaccoIndustry.description: Tobacco industry pioneered the model of funding academic research to create doubt about health risks
tobaccoIndustry.masterSettlementAgreement: After the 1998 MSA, tobacco companies continued funding academic research through new intermediary foundations

pharmaceuticalIndustry.description: Drug companies fund university clinical trials, endowed chairs, and continuing medical education that shapes prescribing practices and regulatory outcomes
pharmaceuticalIndustry.pattern: Industry-funded studies systematically more likely to report favorable results than independently funded studies

mediaInfrastructure.title: Political Media Infrastructure & Dark Money Media
mediaInfrastructure.overview: Political media infrastructure extends beyond traditional news to include corporate-owned local TV stations, partisan digital media companies, and dark money-funded 'pink slime' news sites that now outnumber real local newspapers. Combined spending on political media infrastructure reaches billions per election cycle.

sinclairBroadcasting.name: Sinclair Broadcast Group (now Sinclair Inc.)
sinclairBroadcasting.networkAffiliations: Largest owner of stations affiliated with Fox, NBC, CBS, ABC
sinclairBroadcasting.politicalAlignment: Widely regarded as politically conservative; mandated identical pro-conservative scripts to be read by local anchors
notableIncidents: 2018: Required anchors to read identical promos criticizing 'fake stories' in media
June 2024: Dozens of anchors delivered identical introduction questioning Biden's mental acuity
Regular must-run conservative commentary segments on local news broadcasts

dailyWire.name: The Daily Wire
dailyWire.valuation: Well north of $1 billion (2023 funding round)
dailyWire.initialFunding: $4.7 million from Farris Wilks (petroleum industry billionaire)
dailyWire.politicalAlignment: Conservative media company; significant social media presence and influence
dailyWire.note: Sought additional investors at $1B+ valuation in 2023-2025

epochTimes.name: The Epoch Times
epochTimes.revenueGrowth: 685% increase from 2019 to 2021, reaching $122 million in 2021
epochTimes.conservativeDonorFunding: $8.4 million in contributions and grants in 2020-2021
epochTimes.politicalActivity: Second-largest funder of pro-Trump Facebook advertising in 2019 (after Trump campaign itself)
disinformationIssues: False claims of fraud in 2020 election

pinkSlimeNewsSites.definition: Partisan websites masquerading as local news, funded by dark money, advocacy groups, and special interests
pinkSlimeNewsSites.comparedToRealNewspapers: 1,265 pink slime sites vs. 1,213 daily newspapers (pink slime now outnumbers real local news)

conservative: Metric Media (1,000+ local news sites across all 50 states)
Star News Digital Media (12 conservative-leaning local outlets)
progressive: The American Independent (501(c)(4) pushing Democratic voters)
Courier Newsroom (10 media outlets, 'pro-democracy news network')

pinkSlimeNewsSites.partisanBreakdown: Eight primary organizations (four conservative, four progressive); majority created by conservative Metric Media
pinkSlimeNewsSites.fundingSources: Deep, often opaque ties to dark money, advocacy groups, and other special interests

darkMoneyInMedia2024.superPacChanneling: $1.3 billion of $1.9 billion flowed through super PACs
darkMoneyInMedia2024.tvAdSpending: $2.2 billion in broadcast TV ads by outside groups
darkMoneyInMedia2024.cumulativeSinceCitizensUnited: $4.3 billion+ in dark money on federal elections since 2010

darkMoneyOverview.title: Dark Money — The Big Picture
darkMoneyOverview.overview: Dark money — political spending by nonprofits that do not disclose their donors — has exploded since Citizens United v. FEC (2010). The 2024 election cycle set a record at $1.9 billion, but the true figure is almost certainly higher due to spending that evades disclosure requirements entirely.
darkMoneyOverview.cumulativeSince2010: $4.3 billion+ in disclosed dark money on federal elections

conservative: Koch network (Americans for Prosperity, Stand Together)
Leonard Leo network (Marble Freedom Trust, 85 Fund, Concord Fund)
liberal: Future Forward (largest single super PAC recipient of dark money in 2024)
League of Conservation Voters dark money affiliates

darkMoneyOverview.underestimation: The $1.9 billion figure necessarily — and perhaps substantially — underestimates the true scale of dark money, as it only captures spending that is eventually disclosed

donorOverlap.description: The same small group of wealthy families and foundations fund multiple organizations across the conservative infrastructure

keyDonorFamilies.name: Barre Seid
keyDonorFamilies.totalDonation: $1.6 billion to Marble Freedom Trust (Leonard Leo)

keyDonorFamilies.name: Hansjoerg Wyss (liberal)
keyDonorFamilies.totalDonation: $245 million to Sixteen Thirty Fund and New Venture Fund since 2016

policyPipeline.description: Ideas flow from think tanks through a structured pipeline to become law
stages: 1. Academic research (Mercatus Center, university-funded programs) produces intellectual framework
2. Think tanks (Heritage, Cato, AEI) translate research into policy proposals
3. ALEC and SPN convert proposals into model legislation templates
4. State legislators introduce model bills in state capitols
5. Federalist Society pipeline places sympathetic judges who uphold the legislation
6. Media infrastructure (Sinclair, Daily Wire, pink slime sites) provides public support narrative
7. Dark money funds the entire process while shielding donor identities

conservativeInfrastructure.spnReach: 64 state-level think tanks, $188 million combined revenue

scaleDifferences.note: While liberals outspent conservatives in dark money in the 2024 cycle ($1.2B vs. $664M), conservative infrastructure has deeper institutional roots spanning 50+ years of think tanks, ALEC model legislation, Federalist Society judicial pipeline, and Koch donor networks

dataSources: OpenSecrets.org (FEC filings, outside spending, dark money tracking)
Brennan Center for Justice (dark money research and analysis)
Center for Media and Democracy / EXPOSEDbyCMD (ALEC, SPN investigations)
Capital Research Center (Arabella Advisors network analysis)
Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington (CREW)
Individual organization annual reports and financial statements
ProPublica investigative reporting (Leonard Leo, Barre Seid)
Axios, The Intercept, Washington Post reporting
